title: Blocking graph-rule evaluation at state transitions
description:
Evaluate graph rules at issue state transitions and block the transition when an enforcing rule fails, closing the bypass where an issue reaches `done` despite failing coverage rules.

## Background

Graph rules currently run only in `jit validate` and gate checkers; the write path runs local rules only. The done transition is therefore unguarded by coverage and reference-integrity rules.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Transitioning an issue evaluates the graph rules applicable to it in its target state; a failing rule with `enforce = true` blocks the transition with exit 4 and prints the findings
- [ ] Non-enforcing failures are reported on the transition without blocking it
- [ ] Transition-time rule evaluation runs over only the affected issue's graph neighborhood slice (the issue plus its transitive dependency closure); building that slice may read the store, since reverse edges are not indexed (amended 2026-06-10 with user approval; a persisted reverse-edge index is future work)
- [ ] Blocked transitions are recorded in the event log
- [ ] Documentation describing where rules run (concepts doc and rules.toml comments) matches the new behavior
